name: Support
description: Triage tickets, draft replies, write help-center articles, score customer health, and build churn-save playbooks. Covers inbox through customer success - drafts only, never sends.
author: Houston

agentSeeds..houston/routines/routines.json:
[{"id": "weekly-support-review", "name": "Monday support review", "description": "Every Monday morning: what shipped last week, what's stuck, what to do this week — rolled up across Inbox, Help Center, Success, and Quality.", "prompt": "Run the Monday support review. Use the review-my-support skill with scope=weekly.

Aggregate everything I produced last week from outputs.json: triage, replies, articles, broadcasts, onboarding / renewal / expansion / save drafts, dossiers, audits, playbooks, VoC, routing updates. Group by domain (Inbox / Help Center / Success / Quality). For each domain, surface (a) what shipped, (b) what's stuck — open threads past response time, promises overdue in followups.json, churn flags opened but not addressed, (c) the single most useful next move.

Write the review to reviews/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log it in outputs.json. This routine always surfaces — I want you to see the review every Monday even if the week was quiet.", "schedule": "0 8 * * 1", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "weekly-helpcenter-digest", "name": "Sunday help-center digest", "description": "Every Sunday evening: ticket volume, top themes, unresolved high-priority, and the single most useful docs gap to write next.", "prompt": "Run the weekly help-center digest. Use the review-my-support skill with scope=help-center-digest.

Read conversations.json counts for the past 7 days, patterns.json top 3 themes, requests.json velocity, and known-issues.json state changes. Surface the single most useful docs gap to write next (read patterns.json filtered to clusters without a matching article in articles/).

Write to digests/{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json. Always surface — a quiet week is also news.", "schedule": "0 18 * * 0", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": false, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-churn-pulse", "name": "Monthly churn risk pulse", "description": "Every 1st of the month: scan all active accounts for new churn signals and open flags that never got addressed. Silent unless a new risk appeared.", "prompt": "Run the monthly churn pulse. Use the look-up-a-customer skill with view=churn-risk across every active account in customers.json.

For each account, scan the last 60 days of conversations for cancellation language, 2+ frustration signals, or a usage cliff (via connected PostHog / Mixpanel if linked). Compare against the most recent churn-flags.json state — only surface NEW flags or previously-open flags that never got a save draft.

Write a summary to reviews/churn-pulse-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json.

If no new flags opened AND no previously-open flag is still unaddressed, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. On the first run (no prior pulse exists), always surface — that's the baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 1 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}, {"id": "monthly-response-time-audit", "name": "Monthly response-time audit", "description": "Every 15th: catches response-time-tier regressions — categories drifting, VIP list stale, first-response breaches trending up. Silent unless something shifted.", "prompt": "Run the monthly response-time audit. Use the check-my-inbox skill with scope=response-time-breach across the full window of the last 30 days.

Read conversations.json filtered to the last 30 days. For each conversation, compare firstResponseAt and resolutionAt against the tier thresholds in context/support-context.md. Compute breach rate per tier. Compare against the previous monthly audit to detect shifts.

Also check: whether domains.inbox.routingCategories matches what triage-a-ticket actually assigned this month, and whether any VIPs in support-context.md have churned or signed up new since last audit.

Write to reviews/response-time-audit-{YYYY-MM-DD}.md and log in outputs.json.

If breach rates are within 10% of last audit AND no routing drift AND VIP list is current, end with ROUTINE_OK so this stays silent. On the first run, always surface — baseline.", "schedule": "0 8 15 * *", "enabled": true, "suppress_when_silent": true, "created_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T00:00:00Z"}]
